dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 8374 IH: Equal Treatment for Farmers Act
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Mr. Harris of North Carolina (for himself, Mr. Harris of Maryland, Ms. Boebert, Mr. Wied, Mr. Harrigan, Mr. Fine, Mr. Brecheen, Mr. Clyde, Mrs. Miller of Illinois, Mr. Perry, Mr. Ogles, Mr. Steube, Mr. Collins, Mr. Nehls, Mr. Carter of Georgia, Ms. Letlow, Mr. Burchett, Mr. Self, Mr. Roy, Mr. Norman, Mr. Moore of Alabama, Mr. Van Orden, Mr. Cline, and Mr. Smith of Nebraska) introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on Agriculture

form.official-title: To strike references to socially disadvantaged farmers and ranchers in Federal law, and for other purposes.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Equal Treatment for Farmers Act.

section.header: Striking references to socially disadvantaged farmers and ranchers in Federal law

subsection.text: Section 524(a)(3) of the Federal Crop Insurance Act (7 U.S.C. 1524(a)(3)) is amended—

paragraph.text: by redesignating subparagraphs (D) through (F) as subparagraphs (C) through (E), respectively.

subsection.text: Section 210A of the Agricultural Marketing Act of 1946 (7 U.S.C. 1627c) is amended—

subparagraph.text: by striking paragraph (11); and
by redesignating paragraphs (12) and (13) as paragraphs (11) and (12), respectively;
by striking subclause (II); and
by redesignating subclauses (III) and (IV) as subclauses (II) and (III), respectively; and
in the subclause heading, by striking Beginning, veteran, and socially disadvantaged and inserting Beginning and veteran; and
by striking beginning, veteran, and socially disadvantaged and inserting beginning and veteran.

subsection.text: The Consolidated Farm and Rural Development Act (7 U.S.C. 1921 et seq.) is amended—

subparagraph.text: in subsection (d)(1), by striking and socially disadvantaged farmers or ranchers; and
in subsection (e)(2), by striking socially disadvantaged farmer or rancher or a;
by striking paragraph (11); and
by redesignating paragraphs (12) and (13) as paragraphs (11) and (12), respectively;
in subsection (d)(4)—

clause.text: in subparagraph (A), by adding or at the end;
by striking subparagraph (B);
by redesignating subparagraph (C) as subparagraph (B); and
in subparagraph (B), as so redesignated, by striking ; and at the end; and

subparagraph.text: in subsection (e)—

clause.text: in paragraph (1), by adding and at the end;
by striking paragraph (2); and
by redesignating paragraph (3) as paragraph (2);

subparagraph.text: in the section heading, by striking and socially disadvantaged farmer or rancher;
in subsection (a), by striking or socially disadvantaged farmer or rancher (as defined in section 355(e)(2));
in subsection (b)(1), by striking or socially disadvantaged farmer or rancher;
in subsection (c)(1), by striking or socially disadvantaged farmer or rancher; and
in subsection (f), by striking and Socially Disadvantaged Farmer or Rancher each place it appears;

in subsection (b)(2), by striking socially disadvantaged farmers and ranchers (as defined in subsection (a) of section 2501 of the Food, Agriculture, Conservation, and Trade Act of 1990 (7 U.S.C. 2279)) or; and
by amending subsection (d) to read as follows:

subsection.text: In making loans under subsection (a), the Secretary shall give preference to eligible entities in States that have adopted a statute consisting of an enactment or adoption of the Uniform Partition of Heirs Property Act, as approved and recommended for enactment in all States by the National Conference of Commissioners on Uniform State Laws in 2010, that relend to owners of heirs property (as defined in that Act).

paragraph.text: in section 333B(c)(3)(A) (7 U.S.C. 1983b(c)(3)(A)), by striking , including, as appropriate, socially disadvantaged farmers or ranchers (as defined in section 355(e)(2));
in section 352(c) (7 U.S.C. 2000(c)), by amending paragraph (4) to read as follows:
The period of occupancy allowed the prior owner of homestead property under this section shall be the period requested in writing by the prior owner, except that such period shall not exceed 5 years.

subsection.text: Section 5413 of the Agriculture Improvement Act of 2018 (7 U.S.C. 2008x) is amended—

paragraph.text: in subsection (b)(1)(C), by striking and socially disadvantaged; and
in subsection (c)(1)(B), by striking and socially disadvantaged.

subsection.text: Section 2501 of the Food, Agriculture, Conservation, and Trade Act of 1990 (7 U.S.C. 2279) is amended—

subparagraph.text: by striking paragraphs (5) and (6); and
by redesignating paragraph (7) as paragraph (5);

paragraph.text: in subsection (b), by striking socially disadvantaged farmers and ranchers, veteran farmers and ranchers, and inserting veteran farmers and ranchers;

subparagraph.text: in the subsection heading, by striking socially disadvantaged and;
in paragraph (1)—

clause.text: in subparagraph (A)—

subclause.text: in clause (i), by striking socially disadvantaged farmers and ranchers and; and
in clause (ii), by striking socially disadvantaged farmers or ranchers and;

clause.text: by striking subparagraphs (E) and (F); and
by redesignating subparagraph (G) as subparagraph (E);

subparagraph.text: in paragraph (3)(B)(i), by striking socially disadvantaged farmers or ranchers and;
in paragraph (4)—

clause.text: in subparagraph (A), by striking socially disadvantaged farmers and ranchers and;
in subparagraph (D)—

subclause.text: by redesignating clauses (v) through (vii) as clauses (iii) through (v), respectively;
in clause (iv), as so redesignated, by striking and socially disadvantaged farmers or ranchers; and
in clause (v), as so redesignated, by striking clause (vi) and inserting clause (iv); and

clause.text: in subparagraph (F), by striking socially disadvantaged farmers and ranchers or; and

subparagraph.text: by striking paragraph (5);
by striking paragraph (2);
by redesignating paragraphs (3) through (7) as paragraphs (2) through (6), respectively; and
in paragraph (3), as so redesignated—

clause.text: in subparagraph (A), by adding or at the end;
by striking subparagraph (B); and
by redesignating subparagraph (C) as subparagraph (B);

paragraph.text: in subsection (g)(2), by striking socially disadvantaged farmers and ranchers or;
by redesignating subsections (i), (k), and (l) as subsections (h), (i), and (j), respectively;

subparagraph.text: in paragraph (1)(A), by striking and members of socially disadvantaged groups; and
in paragraph (2)—

clause.text: in subparagraph (A), by striking and members of socially disadvantaged groups;
in subparagraph (B), by adding and at the end;
by striking subparagraphs (C) and (E);
by redesignating subparagraph (D) as subparagraph (C); and
in subparagraph (C), as so redesignated, by striking ; and and inserting a period; and

paragraph.text: in subsection (j)(4)(A), as redesignated by paragraph (7)—

subparagraph.text: in clause (i), by adding and at the end;
by striking clause (ii); and
by redesignating clause (iii) as clause (ii).

subsection.text: The Federal Crop Insurance Reform and Department of Agriculture Reorganization Act of 1994 (7 U.S.C. 6901 et seq.) is amended—

subparagraph.text: in subsection (a), by striking paragraph (3);
in subsection (b)(1)(B)—

clause.text: by striking clause (iii); and
by redesignating clauses (iv) and (v) as clauses (iii) and (iv), respectively;

subparagraph.text: in subsection (c)—

clause.text: in the matter preceding paragraph (1), by striking veteran farmers and ranchers, and socially disadvantaged farmers or ranchers and inserting and veteran farmers and ranchers;
in paragraph (1), by striking socially disadvantaged,; and
in paragraph (5), by striking veteran farmers or ranchers, and socially disadvantaged farmers or ranchers and inserting and veteran farmers or ranchers;

subparagraph.text: by striking subsection (d); and
by redesignating subsections (e) and (f) as subsections (d) and (e), respectively; and

subsection.text: Section 201 of division HH of the Consolidated Appropriations Act, 2023 (Public Law 117–328; 136 Stat. 5971) is repealed.
Section 196 of the Federal Agriculture Improvement and Reform Act of 1996 (7 U.S.C. 7333) is amended—

paragraph.text: in subsection (k)(2), by striking , beginning, or socially disadvantaged and inserting or beginning; and

subparagraph.text: in the paragraph heading, by striking veteran, and socially disadvantaged and inserting and veteran; and
by striking , beginning, and socially disadvantaged and inserting and beginning.

subsection.text: Section 405(c) of the Agricultural Research, Extension, and Education Reform Act of 1998 (7 U.S.C. 7625(c)) is amended by striking socially disadvantaged farmers,.
Section 9011 of the Farm Security and Rural Investment Act of 2002 (7 U.S.C. 8111) is amended—

paragraph.text: in subsection (a), by striking paragraph (9); and

subparagraph.text: in paragraph (2)(B), by amending clause (v) to read as follows:

clause.text: the participation rate by beginning farmers or ranchers (as defined in accordance with section 343(a) of the Consolidated Farm and Rural Development Act (7 U.S.C. 1991(a)));

subparagraph.text: in paragraph (5), by amending subparagraph (B) to read as follows:

The amount of an establishment payment under this subsection shall be not more than 50 percent of the costs of establishing an eligible perennial crop covered by the contract but not to exceed $500 per acre, including—

clause.text: the cost of seeds and stock for perennials;
the cost of planting the perennial crop, as determined by the Secretary; and
in the case of nonindustrial private forestland, the costs of site preparation and tree planting.

subsection.text: The Food, Conservation, and Energy Act of 2008 (7 U.S.C. 8701 et seq.) is amended—

paragraph.text: in section 1101(d) (7 U.S.C. 8711(d)), by amending paragraph (2) to read as follows:
Paragraph (1) shall not apply to a farm owned by a limited resource farmer or rancher, as defined by the Secretary.
in section 1302(d) (7 U.S.C. 8752(d)), by amending paragraph (2) to read as follows:
Paragraph (1) shall not apply to a farm owned by a limited resource farmer or rancher, as defined by the Secretary.

subsection.text: The Agricultural Act of 2014 (7 U.S.C. 9001 et seq.) is amended—

subparagraph.text: by striking subparagraph (A); and
by redesignating subparagraphs (B) through (D) as subparagraphs (A) through (C), respectively;

paragraph.text: in section 1404(c)(4) (7 U.S.C. 9054(c)(4)), by striking veteran, or socially disadvantaged and inserting or veteran; and

subparagraph.text: in clause (i), by adding or at the end;
by striking clause (ii); and
by redesignating clause (iii) as clause (ii).

subsection.text: Subclause (III) of section 8(b)(5)(B)(iii) of the Soil Conservation and Domestic Allotment Act (16 U.S.C. 590h(b)(5)(B)(iii)) is amended to read as follows:

subclause.text: To be eligible for nomination and election to the applicable county, area, or local committee, as determined by the Secretary, an agricultural producer shall be located within the area under the jurisdiction of a county, area, or local committee, and participate or cooperate in programs administered within that area.

subsection.text: Section 402A(b) of the Agricultural Credit Act of 1978 (16 U.S.C. 2202a(b)) is amended by striking , a socially disadvantaged farmer or rancher (as defined in subsection (a) of section 2501 of the Food, Agriculture, Conservation, and Trade Act of 1990 (7 U.S.C. 2279),.
Section 623 of the Agricultural Credit Act of 1987 (7 U.S.C. 1985 note) is repealed.
The Food Security Act of 1985 (Public Law 99–198; 99 Stat. 1504) is amended—

subparagraph.text: by striking paragraph (23); and
by redesignating paragraphs (24) through (27) as paragraphs (23) through (26), respectively;
in the subparagraph heading, by striking socially disadvantaged,;
in the matter preceding clause (i), by striking socially disadvantaged,; and
in clause (i)(II), by striking socially disadvantaged,;

paragraph.text: in section 1240B(d)(4)(A) (16 U.S.C. 3839aa–2(d)(4)(A)), by striking socially disadvantaged farmer or rancher,;
in section 1241(h) (16 U.S.C. 3841(h)), by amending paragraph (1) to read as follows:

subparagraph.text: Of the funds made available for each of fiscal years 2009 through 2018 to carry out the environmental quality incentives program and the acres made available for each of such fiscal years to carry out the conservation stewardship program, the Secretary shall use, to the maximum extent practicable, 5 percent to assist beginning farmers or ranchers.

Of the funds made available for each of fiscal years 2019 through 2031 to carry out the environmental quality incentives program under subchapter A of chapter 4 of subtitle D and the conservation stewardship program under subchapter B of chapter 4 of subtitle D, the Secretary shall use, to the maximum extent practicable, 5 percent to assist beginning farmers or ranchers.

paragraph.text: in section 1271E(d) (16 U.S.C. 3871e(d)), by striking socially disadvantaged farmers and ranchers,.

section.header: Prohibition on racial and gender preferences in USDA programs
section.text: Notwithstanding any other law, no program of the Department of Agriculture may provide any preference, priority consideration, or enhanced benefits to any individual or entity on the basis of race or gender.
